topNotes: Skip Calangute. Stay in Aldona or Assagao if you can afford the extra ₹500/night.
Scooter rental from a women-run garage in Mapusa was cheaper and less hassle than the beach-shack guys.
Curlie's at sunset is still worth it. Get there at 5:30, leave by 9 — after that the crowd changes.
highlight: The Project Café in Aldona on a Wednesday afternoon — empty pool, a pile of art books, three other solo women already there.

topNotes: Tapovan side > Lakshman Jhula side for solo women. Quieter, fewer hawkers, better cafés.
Volvo from Delhi (overnight) saved a hotel night and ₹2,500.
Don't pay for a Ganga aarti 'guided experience'. Just turn up at Triveni Ghat at 6pm.
highlight: Sunrise Ganga walk from Tapovan to Lakshman Jhula — alone for 40 minutes, then 80 monkeys.

topNotes: Composite Ticket is the only smart move — ₹400 for 5 monuments, valid 2 days.
Pink City Rickshaw Company (women drivers) is worth every rupee for a half-day Old City tour.
Anokhi Café is the breakfast you want after a 6am Amber Fort trip. Not a tourist trap.
highlight: Block-printing workshop at the Anokhi Museum. Three hours, two new friends, one ruined kurta.

topNotes: Volvo Delhi → Bhuntar overnight, then shared cab to Kasol. ₹1,800 total, beats flying.
Stayhappy Hostel in Kasol has the only female-only dorm I trust in the valley.
Tosh village hike is doable solo in May. Don't try in monsoon.
highlight: Reading on the rocks above the Parvati at 7am with no one within shouting distance.

topNotes: Stay on the Hippie Island side (Virupapur Gaddi). Hampi Bazaar side is dry, no alcohol, by 9pm dead.
Rent a moped from Hampi Bazaar — boulders are too far apart to walk.
Sunrise at Matanga Hill is worth the 5am climb. Sunset at Hemakuta is the easier alternative.
highlight: Sitting alone in the Achyutaraya temple courtyard for 90 minutes. Not a soul. Late February is the secret.

topNotes: ₹4 government ferry between Fort Kochi and Ernakulam — 25 minutes, sunset view, unbeatable.
Skip the houseboat overnight scam. Day trip from Alleppey is enough and ₹4,000 cheaper.
Kashi Art Café is overhyped. Kayees Biryani is not.
highlight: A solo Kathakali show at Kerala Kathakali Centre. Front row, ₹350, makeup demo at 5pm.

topNotes: Stay in Hanuman Ghat side, not Lal Ghat. Same view, half the price, zero touts.
Sadhna Collective HQ visit is a 30-minute auto from the city — and the heart of women-led Rajasthan.
Avoid the big-name lake palace boat ride. The ₹400 government ferry to Jagmandir does the job.
highlight: Sunrise from the cliff above Bagore Ki Haveli. Bring chai. No one else is up there at 6am.

topNotes: Skip the Pink City. Spend two days in Amer (the village, not just the fort) and Nahargarh.
Eat at LMB only once. Then go where I go: Sahu Chai for breakfast, Rawat for kachori.
Block printing in Bagru (45 min from Jaipur) is more rewarding than Sanganer.
highlight: Watching Holika Dahan at Hawa Mahal Bazaar from a friend's terrace. No tourists. Just neighbors.

topNotes: Anand Prakash Yoga Ashram is the only TTC I'd send a beginner to. Avoid the Tapovan strip mills.
₹6/day Ganga ferry (not the tourist boat) is the secret crossing.
Pre-book your YTT in October. By February the good ones are gone.
highlight: First handstand in 11 years on a sandbank by Ram Jhula at sunrise. No witnesses.

topNotes: Stay at Hostel LaVie's female dorm — the only one I trust on the ghats.
Take the female-rowed sunrise boat (collective near Assi Ghat) instead of the tourist boatmen.
Brown Bread Bakery is base camp. Set up your day from there with chai and WiFi.
highlight: Aarti at Assi Ghat from a rooftop with three Israeli women I'd met that morning at Brown Bread.

destination: A weekend in Bangalore (when you live in Bangalore)
topNotes: Lahe Lahe in Indiranagar on a Saturday morning = tea + a women-only writing circle.
Champaca Books in Vasanth Nagar is the bookstore I send everyone to.
Skip the breweries on Church Street. Try Toit only on a Tuesday.
highlight: A four-hour solo brunch at Cinnamon, with one chai too many and zero apologies.

topNotes: Hauz Khas Village > Paharganj for a returning foreigner. Pay the extra ₹1,500/night.
Metro is the cheat code. ₹70 for a day pass beats every Uber I've taken in Delhi.
Old Delhi food walk with a women-led tour (Storytrails or similar) is worth the ₹2,500.
highlight: An afternoon at the Sunder Nursery in February — 90 acres, almost empty, ₹35 entry.
